station_address: S.V.Road, Malad, Somwari Bazar, Malad East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400064
text:
I visited the IndianOil Petrol Pump in Dahisar East about two weeks ago, and the experience was average.
The fuel filling process was smooth, and there was no major waiting time. Staff members were generally polite, though not particularly proactive. Basic instructions were followed, but customer interaction felt minimal.
The premises were moderately clean, but maintenance could be improved — especially around the air filling and waiting areas. During peak hours, the station felt slightly congested, which made vehicle movement a bit inconvenient.
Fuel measurement appeared transparent, and digital payment options were available, which is a positive point.
Overall, a decent and functional petrol pump. It gets the job done, but improvements in cleanliness, crowd management, and customer engagement would enhance the experience.

station_address: S.V.Road, Malad, Somwari Bazar, Malad East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400064
text:
Scam

My wife had requested petrol for ₹300, but the staff stopped the meter at ₹200.
When she questioned it, 2nd scam the person quickly entered another ₹100 manually, without the meter running.

Wow! Only when she insisted and pointed out the issue, the staff accepted what they had done and apologize.

station_address: S.V.Road, Malad, Somwari Bazar, Malad East, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400064
text: I had an extremely disappointing experience at this petrol pump. The staff is involved in fraudulent practices. When I asked for a full tank, the worker pretended not to hear and only filled around 200 rupees worth of petrol. When I pointed it out, they tried to convince me that I had asked for only that amount, and multiple staff members backed up the claim. When I insisted on a full tank, they added the 200 rupees to the total, making it look like I had been charged twice. This is a clear scam and I would strongly advise everyone to stay away from this petrol pump.
